candidates.thesis: When the close decisively pushes into the top decile of its multi-week high-low range after being in the lower half, buyers have seized control.
candidates.code:
def signal(d, params):
    rng_n = params.get('rng_n', 25)
    hold = params.get('hold', 5)
    hi_thr = params.get('hi_thr', 0.85)
    lo_thr = params.get('lo_thr', 0.45)
    c = d['close']
    hh = d['high'].rolling(rng_n, min_periods=rng_n // 2).max()
    ll = d['low'].rolling(rng_n, min_periods=rng_n // 2).min()
    pos = (c - ll) / (hh - ll).replace(0, np.nan)
    was_low = pos.shift(2) < lo_thr
    now_high = pos > hi_thr
    fire = (was_low & now_high & (pos.shift(1) <= hi_thr)).fillna(False)
    return _hold(fire.to_numpy(), hold, c.index)

candidates.thesis: When the second difference of smoothed price turns positive while momentum is rising, the move is accelerating and tends to continue.
candidates.code:
def signal(d, params):
    smooth = params.get('smooth', 5)
    accel_n = params.get('accel_n', 3)
    hold = params.get('hold', 4)
    trend = params.get('trend', 100)
    c = d['close']
    sm = c.ewm(span=smooth, adjust=False).mean()
    vel = sm - sm.shift(accel_n)
    accel = vel - vel.shift(accel_n)
    above = c > c.rolling(trend, min_periods=trend // 2).mean()
    fire = ((accel > 0) & (vel > 0) & (accel.shift(accel_n) <= 0) & above).fillna(False)
    return _hold(fire.to_numpy(), hold, c.index)
candidates.reason: improves the blend but corr too high

candidates.thesis: After a steady decline, the moment price velocity stops getting more negative and inflects upward marks the bottoming inflection point.
candidates.code:
def signal(d, params):
    smooth = params.get('smooth', 6)
    n = params.get('n', 4)
    hold = params.get('hold', 6)
    rsi_n = params.get('rsi_n', 14)
    rsi_max = params.get('rsi_max', 45)
    c = d['close']
    sm = c.ewm(span=smooth, adjust=False).mean()
    vel = sm - sm.shift(n)
    accel = vel - vel.shift(n)
    declining = vel.shift(n) < 0
    inflect = (accel > 0) & (accel.shift(1) <= 0)
    r = _rsi(c, rsi_n)
    fire = (declining & inflect & (r < rsi_max)).fillna(False)
    return _hold(fire.to_numpy(), hold, c.index)

candidates.thesis: A large gap up after an extended run that immediately fades intraday reflects exhausted buyers and reverts over the next few days.
candidates.code:
def signal(d, params):
    gap_atr = params.get('gap_atr', 1.0)
    run_n = params.get('run_n', 10)
    atr_n = params.get('atr', 14)
    hold = params.get('hold', 4)
    c = d['close']
    pc = c.shift(1)
    atr = _atr(d, atr_n)
    gap = (d['open'] - pc) / atr
    extended = c.shift(1) > c.shift(run_n + 1)
    intraday_fade = c < d['open']
    closed_red = c < pc
    fire = ((gap > gap_atr) & extended & intraday_fade & closed_red).fillna(False)
    return _hold(fire.to_numpy(), hold, c.index)
candidates.reason: does not improve the blended book (the ensemble already owns this)

candidates.thesis: A day with a long lower wick relative to its body and range reveals aggressive demand absorbing selling, often preceding a multi-day bounce.
candidates.code:
def signal(d, params):
    wick_frac = params.get('wick_frac', 0.55)
    atr_n = params.get('atr', 14)
    hold = params.get('hold', 5)
    rsi_n = params.get('rsi_n', 14)
    rsi_max = params.get('rsi_max', 50)
    c = d['close']
    o = d['open']
    rng = (d['high'] - d['low']).replace(0, np.nan)
    body_low = pd.concat([o, c], axis=1).min(axis=1)
    lower_wick = (body_low - d['low']) / rng
    closed_up = c > o
    r = _rsi(c, rsi_n)
    fire = ((lower_wick > wick_frac) & closed_up & (r < rsi_max)).fillna(False)
    return _hold(fire.to_numpy(), hold, c.index)
candidates.reason: does not improve the blended book (the ensemble already owns this)

candidates.thesis: An inside day signals a coiled balance; an upside breakout of the mother bar's high resolves that balance bullishly.
candidates.code:
def signal(d, params):
    hold = params.get('hold', 4)
    atr_n = params.get('atr', 14)
    trend = params.get('trend', 100)
    c = d['close']
    inside = (d['high'] < d['high'].shift(1)) & (d['low'] > d['low'].shift(1))
    inside_y = inside.shift(1).fillna(False)
    mother_high = d['high'].shift(2)
    above = c > c.rolling(trend, min_periods=trend // 2).mean()
    fire = (inside_y & (c > mother_high) & above).fillna(False)
    return _hold(fire.to_numpy(), hold, c.index)
candidates.reason: does not improve the blended book (the ensemble already owns this)

candidates.thesis: A close that pokes to a new high but reverses below the prior breakout level is a bull trap; we instead buy the subsequent reclaim of that level.
candidates.code:
def signal(d, params):
    hi_n = params.get('hi_n', 30)
    atr_n = params.get('atr', 14)
    hold = params.get('hold', 5)
    c = d['close']
    prior_hi = d['high'].rolling(hi_n, min_periods=hi_n // 2).max().shift(1)
    poked = (d['high'] > prior_hi)
    failed = poked & (c < prior_hi)
    failed_y = failed.shift(1).fillna(False)
    reclaim = c > prior_hi
    fire = (failed_y & reclaim).fillna(False)
    return _hold(fire.to_numpy(), hold, c.index)
candidates.reason: does not improve the blended book (the ensemble already owns this)

candidates.thesis: A cluster of unusually small true-range days reflects stored energy; the first day true range expands with an up close releases it upward.
candidates.code:
def signal(d, params):
    tr_n = params.get('tr_n', 10)
    ref = params.get('ref', 60)
    low_pct = params.get('low_pct', 0.25)
    exp_mult = params.get('exp_mult', 1.5)
    hold = params.get('hold', 5)
    c = d['close']
    pc = c.shift(1)
    tr = pd.concat([(d['high'] - d['low']), (d['high'] - pc).abs(), (d['low'] - pc).abs()], axis=1).max(axis=1)
    avg_tr = tr.rolling(tr_n, min_periods=tr_n // 2).mean()
    thr = tr.rolling(ref, min_periods=ref // 2).quantile(low_pct)
    compressed = avg_tr.shift(1) < thr.shift(1)
    expand = (tr > avg_tr.shift(1) * exp_mult) & (c > pc)
    fire = (compressed & expand).fillna(False)
    return _hold(fire.to_numpy(), hold, c.index)
candidates.reason: does not improve the blended book (the ensemble already owns this)

candidates.thesis: When price dips below a rolling support level then closes back above it, the failed breakdown traps shorts and triggers a snap-back.
candidates.code:
def signal(d, params):
    sup_n = params.get('sup_n', 20)
    buf_atr = params.get('buf_atr', 0.25)
    atr_n = params.get('atr', 14)
    hold = params.get('hold', 6)
    c = d['close']
    atr = _atr(d, atr_n)
    sup = d['low'].rolling(sup_n, min_periods=sup_n // 2).min().shift(1)
    broke = d['low'] < (sup - buf_atr * atr)
    reclaim = c > sup
    fire = (broke & reclaim).fillna(False)
    return _hold(fire.to_numpy(), hold, c.index)
candidates.reason: does not improve the blended book (the ensemble already owns this)

candidates.thesis: A gap down that partially fills intraday but stays below prior close shows dip-buying interest, and the unfilled gap acts as a magnet over coming days.
candidates.code:
def signal(d, params):
    gap_atr = params.get('gap_atr', 0.6)
    atr_n = params.get('atr', 14)
    hold = params.get('hold', 5)
    trend = params.get('trend', 150)
    c = d['close']
    pc = c.shift(1)
    atr = _atr(d, atr_n)
    gap = (pc - d['open']) / atr
    recovered = c > d['open']
    still_below = c < pc
    above = c > c.rolling(trend, min_periods=trend // 2).mean()
    fire = ((gap > gap_atr) & recovered & still_below & above).fillna(False)
    return _hold(fire.to_numpy(), hold, c.index)
candidates.reason: does not improve the blended book (the ensemble already owns this)

candidates.thesis: The narrowest high-low range of recent days marks indecision; a close above that bar's high resolves the indecision in the bullish direction.
candidates.code:
def signal(d, params):
    nr_n = params.get('nr_n', 7)
    hold = params.get('hold', 5)
    trend = params.get('trend', 120)
    c = d['close']
    rng = d['high'] - d['low']
    is_narrow = rng <= rng.rolling(nr_n, min_periods=nr_n // 2).min()
    nr_high = d['high'].where(is_narrow)
    last_nr_high = nr_high.ffill().shift(1)
    above = c > c.rolling(trend, min_periods=trend // 2).mean()
    fire = ((c > last_nr_high) & (c.shift(1) <= last_nr_high) & above).fillna(False)
    return _hold(fire.to_numpy(), hold, c.index)
candidates.reason: does not improve the blended book (the ensemble already owns this)

candidates.thesis: An opening gap up that holds above its prior close through the close signals informed buying that tends to persist for several days.
candidates.code:
def signal(d, params):
    gap_atr = params.get('gap_atr', 0.5)
    hold = params.get('hold', 5)
    atr_n = params.get('atr', 14)
    vol_mult = params.get('vol_mult', 1.2)
    vol_n = params.get('vol_n', 20)
    c = d['close']
    pc = c.shift(1)
    atr = _atr(d, atr_n)
    gap = (d['open'] - pc) / atr
    vol_ok = d['volume'] > d['volume'].rolling(vol_n, min_periods=vol_n // 2).mean() * vol_mult
    held = c > pc
    fire = ((gap > gap_atr) & held & vol_ok).fillna(False)
    return _hold(fire.to_numpy(), hold, c.index)
candidates.reason: does not improve the blended book (the ensemble already owns this)
